

<div style="text-align: left;">
  <img src="assets/logo/DRSN_logo_smaller.png" alt="Image" style="width: 2in; float: left; margin-right: 10px;">
  <div style="overflow: hidden; padding-top: 8%;">

## **Xenium Analysis Pipeline**    
<br>
<hr>
</div> 
<br>

## 

This page documents the progress of the pipeline for 10x Genomics Xenium <i>in-situ</i> transcriptomics data. It covers the full workflow from raw Xenium Ranger output through to differential expression and spatial analysis. Each module defines its inputs, outputs, and key decision points. A dictionary section covers steps that require contextual reasoning rather than fixed execution.

## Technology Readiness Levels

```{r trl-table, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=F}
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

trl <- data.frame(
  TRL = 7:0,
  Description = c(
    "Pipeline is Scientific Platforms compatible",
    "Old data run through whole pipeline; details documented and pipeline revisited",
    "Integrate into a FAIR framework",
    "Revised pipeline validated on 2-3 old or new datasets",
    "Old data run through whole pipeline; details documented and pipeline revisited",
    "Pipeline designed and fully detailed; draft code per module developed",
    "Code repository available; broad pipeline described",
    "No established code base"
  ),
  Actions = c(
    "Independently deployable on 3 new projects; training and education materials developed; pipeline handed off; core facility ready; cost-recovery metadata collected",
    "Reproduce known biological results on benchmark/public dataset; marker gene or cell type recovery meets defined threshold",
    "Reproducible report designed; balances efficient outputs with interpretability; meets expectations for data sharing and transparency",
    "Predict and document failure points (predictability is a feature, not a bug); communications developed for user-facing handoff",
    "Use available data; run through pipeline; identify and document failure points; inform pipeline design changes; code instructions developed",
    "Complete first draft overview pipeline with detailed pages; describe touch points and input/output; Brian approved; modules developed, functioned and documented",
    "Document reference material in repository; design overview pipeline visualisation",
    "Find existing repositories or create repository; establish a codebase"
  ),
  Status = c("⬜",
             "⬜",
             "⬜",
             "⬜",
             "⬜",
             "⬜",
             "🔄",
             "✅")
)
trl <- trl[nrow(trl):1, ]

kable(trl, col.names = c("TRL", "Description", "Actions", "Status"),
      align = c("c", "l", "l", "c"), row.names = F) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE,
                font_size = 13) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "3em", bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "20em") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "30em") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "3em") %>%
  row_spec(which(trl$Status == "📍"), background = "#fff9e6")
```

## Toolbox

**Functions**:  
- Image-Prep for `cellpose` segmentation from a directory of Xenium outs folders (not documented; n=1; python). [pageX]()  
- Rapid regeneration of Seurat Object from custom mask (not documented; n=1; R/python). [pageX]() 

**Workflows**:  
- Data-informed merging of custom cell segmentation (not documented; n=1; R/python). [pageX]() 

<!---Change Log--->

<details><summary>**Change Log:**</summary>

<hr>

<div class = "hint-goals">  
**2026-03-30** Reference data to download  
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE312420  
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE312415  
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE284742  
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE307895 PMID: 41252205
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE300147
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE270678 VISIUM
</div>


<hr>

<div class = "hint-goals">  
**2026-03-25** Update (but not organised)  
I currently have  
a) python function for Xenium-specific image preparations for cellpose segmentation.  
b) python script for pulling transcript data and generating a new `.mtx`, `barcodes.tsv` and `features.tsv` from a custom segmentation.  
c) have a workflow for data-informed custom merge segmentation.  
**Need to now document these and ingest those into this repo**...  
Next, need to have a custom-into-Seurat vs XeniumRanger-into-Seurat workflow. Working on this now.   
</div>

<hr>

<div class = "hint-goals">  
**2026-03-18** Xenium Pipeline page created.  
First update!  
</div>

</details>
<br>
